title: Unit 1 — Talking my language
subtitle: Article, Coursebook exercises 8–9 and Workbook page 6

blocks.title: The Google translators in human form
paragraphs: Michael Erard's new book investigates the master linguists or 'hyperglots'. By Liam Scanlon.

The 18th century Italian priest Cardinal Giuseppe Mezzofanti is a legend among linguists. They say he studied 72 languages, 30 of which he mastered. He spoke another nine fluently, though not perfectly, and could hold a basic conversation in at least eleven more. And all that without leaving Italy! One story suggests he picked up Ukrainian in just two weeks, after meeting a visitor from there.

But how far is this true? Certainly, the figure of 72 is too high and some people perhaps exaggerated how fluent he was. He lived at a time when travel was difficult and learning other languages was still unusual. Therefore, many reports of his abilities come from visitors who were probably struggling to express themselves in Italian. There were also those who, while appreciating his good accent and accurate grammar, described him as merely a parrot who said nothing of interest. However, according to Michael Erard, author of the book Mezzofanti's Gift, there is sufficient evidence to believe he could use many languages.

Erard also argues that there are many hyperglots in the world today and that, with globalisation, there will be a growing number in the future. For example, Alexander Arguelles is fluent in around twenty languages and has studied 60. He studies nine hours a day, down from fourteen before he got married! The Hungarian translator Kato Lomb worked with sixteen, and you can watch a YouTube video of Alex Rawling speaking eleven languages at the age of twenty.

A central question of the book is whether hyperglots are born or made. Are their achievements genetic or do hyperglots have secrets that normal language learners can learn from? Erard's conclusions agree with research on highly talented people in other areas such as sport and music. These people generally have advantages they are born with: top athletes may have genes that allow them to get the most from their training; hyperglots seem to possess excellent memories and have brains that are more efficient in processing speech sounds. However, becoming the best also requires a lot of hard work. Some argue that the difference between a top performer and someone who's just 'very good' is that the top performer has practised for 10,000 hours instead of 6,000.

The fact is that most ordinary language learners lack these natural advantages and simply don't have that much time. So is there any hope for us? Erard believes there is and that research on hyperglots can offer some useful lessons. For example, they often have limited ambitions in terms of individual languages: they're happy to get by, or to be only able to read, or not to have a perfect accent. They're practical: if they can't travel, they look for opportunities to use the language closer to home. Some simply imagine conversations in their heads. They also use other techniques like learning words in context. Finally, they're never afraid to make mistakes or appear stupid and so never give up.

blocks.title: Coursebook exercise 8
paragraphs: Find these words in the article and notice the connected words and grammar: far, evidence, growing, allow, terms, opportunities.
Copy the connected phrase from the article.

blocks.question: far — copy the connected phrase from the article.
acceptedAnswers: how far is this true
But how far is this true?
But how far is this true

blocks.question: evidence — copy the connected phrase from the article.
acceptedAnswers: sufficient evidence to believe
there is sufficient evidence to believe

blocks.question: growing — copy the connected phrase from the article.
acceptedAnswers: a growing number
there will be a growing number

blocks.question: allow — copy the connected phrase from the article.
blocks.correctAnswer: allow them to get the most from their training
acceptedAnswers: allow them to get the most from their training
genes that allow them to get the most from their training

blocks.question: terms — copy the connected phrase from the article.
acceptedAnswers: in terms of
limited ambitions in terms of individual languages

blocks.question: opportunities — copy the connected phrase from the article.
blocks.correctAnswer: look for opportunities to use the language closer to home
acceptedAnswers: look for opportunities to use the language closer to home
they look for opportunities to use the language closer to home

blocks.title: Coursebook exercise 9
paragraphs: Cover the article and complete the sentences. Then check your ideas.
Write each completed sentence in full.

blocks.question: 1. But ______ far ______ ?
blocks.correctAnswer: But how far is this true?
acceptedAnswers: But how far is this true?
But how far is this true

blocks.question: 2. There ______ evidence ______ he could use many languages.
blocks.correctAnswer: There is sufficient evidence to believe he could use many languages.
acceptedAnswers: There is sufficient evidence to believe he could use many languages.
There is sufficient evidence to believe he could use many languages

blocks.question: 3. There will ______ growing ______ in the future.
blocks.correctAnswer: There will be a growing number in the future.
acceptedAnswers: There will be a growing number in the future.
There will be a growing number in the future

blocks.question: 4. Top ______ may ______ genes ______ allow ______ get the ______ their training.
blocks.correctAnswer: Top athletes may have genes that allow them to get the most from their training.
acceptedAnswers: Top athletes may have genes that allow them to get the most from their training.
Top athletes may have genes that allow them to get the most from their training

blocks.question: 5. They often ______ limited ______ terms ______ individual languages.
blocks.correctAnswer: They often have limited ambitions in terms of individual languages.
acceptedAnswers: They often have limited ambitions in terms of individual languages.
They often have limited ambitions in terms of individual languages

blocks.question: 6. They ______ opportunities ______ language closer to home.
blocks.correctAnswer: They look for opportunities to use the language closer to home.
acceptedAnswers: They look for opportunities to use the language closer to home.
They look for opportunities to use the language closer to home

blocks.title: Workbook page 6 — Vocabulary: Talking about languages
paragraphs: Exercise 1. Replace the underlined words with the expressions in the box.
Word box: get by, fluently, express, mastering, my accent, accurate, picked it up, struggled.

blocks.question: 1a. At first I really ______ speaking French at work.
2a. It's hard to be ______ all the time.
3. I'm not brilliant at German, but I can ______ in the language in most everyday situations.
4. I'm pretty good at Japanese now. I ______ by living in Kyoto for about 10 years.
5. I haven't been studying Italian for very long, so it's hard to ______ what I want to say sometimes.
6. I was born in Greece but I went to an international school, so I speak English ______.

blocks.title: Language note — word order with phrasal verbs
paragraphs: Remember the word order when you use a pronoun with separable verbs.
I picked up my Spanish while travelling. OR I picked my Spanish up while travelling.
BUT: I picked it up while travelling. NOT: I picked up it while travelling.

blocks.title: Workbook exercise 2 — Word types
paragraphs: Look at the underlined words. What type of word is each one?

I've just 1 taken up painting at our local art college. To be honest, I'm no Van Gogh. Doing anything 2 for the first time is 3 hard and people always want to do 4 well. But my 5 advice is to concentrate on the activity. What I 6 mean is, enjoy what 7 you are doing and don't worry about the end result. And never compare yourself with anyone else. There will always be those 8 annoyingly talented people who can 9 take on any new challenge and succeed. Ignore them.

blocks.title: Workbook exercise 3 — Test your knowledge
paragraphs: 1. What's the opposite of these words and expressions? Circle the correct option.

blocks.title: Workbook exercise 3.2 — make or do?
paragraphs: Do these words collocate with make or do?

blocks.question: Choose make or do for each phrase.

blocks.title: Workbook exercise 3.3 — Prepositions
paragraphs: Write the correct prepositions.

blocks.title: Workbook exercise 3.4 — Word stress
paragraphs: Where is the stress on these words?

blocks.question: What do honest, knowledge, write and foreign have in common?
options: They all contain a silent letter
They all start with a vowel sound
They are all borrowed from French
blocks.correctAnswer: They all contain a silent letter
blocks.explanation: honest: silent h; knowledge: silent k; write: silent w; foreign: silent g.

blocks.title: Workbook exercise 4 — Listening 1.1
paragraphs: You are going to hear Trisha and Andy talking about a change in Trisha's life. Choose the correct answers, a, b or c.

blocks.question: 1. What course has Trisha decided to go on?
2. What is Andy's reaction to her news?
options: He wants to try something new too

blocks.question: 3. What was the problem at the first class?
options: All the other students were men
Trisha didn't know the answer to a question

blocks.question: 4. What will Trisha do in the first term?
5. Trisha thinks her new job will be:

vocabulary.title: Unit 1 vocabulary — 30 key words and phrases
vocabulary.label: Unit 1 · 30 key items
vocabulary.description: Words and phrases needed to understand the article and complete the homework without using an external dictionary.

words.ru: гиперполиглот; человек, владеющий очень многими языками
words.exampleEn: The book is about hyperglots who learn many languages.
words.exampleRu: Книга рассказывает о гиперполиглотах, которые учат много языков.

words.exampleEn: The author investigates how people master many languages.
words.exampleRu: Автор исследует, как люди осваивают много языков.

words.exampleEn: Mastering a language can take years.
words.exampleRu: Овладение языком в совершенстве может занять годы.

words.exampleEn: I speak English fluently.
words.exampleRu: Я свободно говорю по-английски.

words.exampleEn: He could hold a basic conversation in at least eleven more languages.
words.exampleRu: Он мог поддержать простой разговор как минимум ещё на одиннадцати языках.

words.exampleEn: He picked up Ukrainian after meeting a visitor.
words.exampleRu: Он освоил украинский после встречи с гостем.

words.exampleEn: Some people perhaps exaggerated how fluent he was.
words.exampleRu: Возможно, некоторые люди преувеличивали, насколько свободно он говорил.

words.exampleEn: Visitors were probably struggling to express themselves in Italian.
words.exampleRu: Вероятно, посетителям было трудно выражать свои мысли по-итальянски.

words.exampleEn: No-one understood my accent.
words.exampleRu: Никто не понимал мой акцент.

words.exampleEn: It is hard to be accurate all the time.
words.exampleRu: Трудно всё время быть точным.

words.exampleEn: There is sufficient evidence to believe he could use many languages.
words.exampleRu: Есть достаточно доказательств, чтобы считать, что он мог использовать много языков.

words.exampleEn: There will be a growing number in the future.
words.exampleRu: В будущем их число будет расти.

words.exampleEn: They get the most from their training.
words.exampleRu: Они получают максимум от своих тренировок.

words.exampleEn: They have limited ambitions in terms of individual languages.
words.exampleRu: У них умеренные цели в отношении отдельных языков.

words.exampleEn: They look for opportunities to use the language closer to home.
words.exampleRu: Они ищут возможности использовать язык ближе к дому.

words.exampleEn: I am not brilliant at German, but I can get by in most everyday situations.
words.exampleRu: Я не блестяще знаю немецкий, но могу справиться в большинстве повседневных ситуаций.

words.en: take up / take on a challenge
words.exampleEn: She took up painting and decided to take on a new challenge.
words.exampleRu: Она начала заниматься живописью и решила принять новый вызов.

words.en: make: a mistake, progress, an effort, a fool of yourself
words.ru: make: допустить ошибку, добиться прогресса, приложить усилие, выставить себя глупо
words.exampleEn: Make an effort, make progress and do not worry about making a mistake.
words.exampleRu: Приложи усилие, добивайся прогресса и не переживай из-за ошибки.

words.en: do: an exercise, homework, a course, a good job
words.ru: do: выполнить упражнение, сделать домашнюю работу, пройти курс, хорошо справиться
words.exampleEn: Do the exercise, finish your homework and do a good job.
words.exampleRu: Выполни упражнение, закончи домашнюю работу и хорошо справься.

words.exampleEn: Do not worry about your progress.
words.exampleRu: Не беспокойся о своём прогрессе.

words.exampleEn: You can rely on your best friend.
words.exampleRu: Ты можешь положиться на своего лучшего друга.

words.exampleEn: Concentrate on the activity.
words.exampleRu: Сосредоточься на самом занятии.

words.exampleEn: Learn how to deal with a problem.
words.exampleRu: Научись справляться с проблемой.

words.en: a basic level / a high level
words.exampleEn: She started at a basic level and later reached a high level.
words.exampleRu: Она начала с базового уровня, а затем достигла высокого.

words.en: a minor mistake / a serious mistake
words.exampleEn: It was only a minor mistake, not a serious one.
words.exampleRu: Это была лишь небольшая ошибка, а не серьёзная.

words.exampleEn: The course begins with general skills and later teaches building skills.
words.exampleRu: Курс начинается с общих навыков, а затем обучает строительным навыкам.

words.exampleEn: Andy is impressed by her news.
words.exampleRu: Энди впечатлён её новостью.

words.exampleEn: Trisha said something silly in the first class.
words.exampleRu: На первом занятии Триша сказала что-то глупое.

words.exampleEn: Will Trisha study bricklaying in the first term?
words.exampleRu: Будет ли Триша изучать кладку кирпича в первом семестре?

words.exampleEn: The job may be hard work, but it could be well paid.
words.exampleRu: Работа может быть тяжёлой, но хорошо оплачиваемой.
